name: Nevada SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Nevada's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Nevada Division of Welfare and Supportive Services (DWSS).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households in Nevada

name: Nevada Medicaid
description: Nevada's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 205% FPL

name: Nevada Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Nevada Department of Employment, Training, and Rehabilitation.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Nevada Housing Division
description: Provides affordable housing resources including down payment assistance, homebuyer programs, rental assistance, and housing development for Nevada residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; income limits based on AMI

name: Nevada Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Nevadans statewide in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below; some programs may have different limits

name: Legal Aid Center of Southern Nevada
description: Provides free civil legal services to low-income residents in southern Nevada including Las Vegas, covering family law, housing, guardianship, and consumer matters.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in Clark County/southern Nevada
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125-200% FPL depending on program

name: EmployNV Career Hubs
description: Nevada's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through career hubs statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Nevada residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Nevada LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through the Nevada Housing Division.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Nevada 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Nevadans with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Nevada residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Three Square Food Bank (Las Vegas)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving southern Nevada including the Las Vegas metro area. Distributes food through a network of 1,600+ partner sites and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southern Nevada

name: Food Bank of Northern Nevada
description: Feeding America affiliate serving northern Nevada and parts of eastern California. Provides food distribution through partner agencies and community programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in northern Nevada

name: Nevada Department of Veterans Services
description: Assists Nevada veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and veterans home care.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Nevada

name: Nevada Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress for Nevada residents.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Nevada experiencing a mental health crisis

name: Nevada Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% SMI

name: Nevada TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance program providing monthly benefits and supportive services to low-income families with children to help achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Nevada WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% FPL

name: RTC (Regional Transportation Commission of Southern Nevada)
description: Public transit system serving the Las Vegas metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service, the Strip and Downtown Express, and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Las Vegas metro area; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and students
hours: Many routes operate 24/7; varies by route

name: RTC Washoe (Regional Transportation Commission of Washoe County)
description: Public transit system serving the Reno-Sparks metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service, rapid transit, and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Reno-Sparks area

name: Nevada Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and HVAC system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Nevada Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Nevadans with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, assistive technology, and career counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Southern Nevada Regional Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Las Vegas area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in southern Nevada
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Reno Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Reno area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Reno area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Nevada Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence through the Nevada Network Against Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Nevada
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Nevada Silver State Opportunity Grant
description: Need-based state grant for Nevada residents attending eligible in-state institutions. Helps cover tuition and fees for low-income students.
eligibilitySummary: Nevada residents attending eligible NSHE institutions who demonstrate significant financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Must have $0 expected family contribution on FAFSA

name: Nevada Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, and English as a Second Language programs at locations across Nevada.
eligibilitySummary: Adults who need basic education, GED preparation, or English language instruction

name: HELP of Southern Nevada
description: Comprehensive social services agency in Las Vegas providing emergency assistance, food, housing, financial counseling, and job training for individuals and families in crisis.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in southern Nevada facing crisis situations
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Community Action Agencies of Nevada
description: Network of community action agencies providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, energy, and workforce development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Nevada
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally 125-200% FPL

name: Nevada Head Start
description: Federally funded early childhood education programs promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Nevada families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Nevada Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Nevada households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Access to Healthcare Network (Reno)
description: Provides affordable healthcare access for uninsured and underinsured northern Nevadans through a network of volunteer providers and community partnerships.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured and underinsured residents of northern Nevada at or below 250% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 250% FPL or below

name: Nevada CHIP (Nevada Check Up)
description: Nevada's Children's Health Insurance Program providing affordable health coverage for uninsured children in families earning too much for Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below CHIP thresholds
incomeLimitNotes: Children in families up to 205% FPL

name: Nevada Rent and Utility Assistance
description: Emergency rental and utility assistance for Nevada tenants to prevent eviction and maintain housing stability during financial hardship.
eligibilitySummary: Renters who have experienced financial hardship and are at risk of eviction or housing instability
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% AMI

name: United Way of Southern Nevada
description: Provides community programs and funding for education, financial stability, and health services in the Las Vegas and southern Nevada area.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of the southern Nevada region
